items.title: [正面] This distinguishes itself as a gacha by allowing d...
items.summary:
This distinguishes itself as a gacha by allowing dead characters to be playable - that opens up a lot of options story-wise, and the story is pretty fantastic here. The devs put a lot of care into it, and the finer details - imagery, pacing, etc. - are all on point, though the macro-narrative feels a bit shallow as of c.6. A more critical person might call it sympathy bait in the way it creates interesting characters to kill them off - similar to the film "Dancer in the Dark", which has been criticized on those grounds. Still, it's a narrative loop I do enjoy.

The other unique aspect is how nice the gacha feels - the devs give away a lot of pulls, and the effective SSR pull rate (with pity) is 5%. The big caveat is that you do need dupes if you want some characters to be good, and the game does get challenging enough to need that, but it nonetheless feels nice being able to get one copy of most characters while vertically investing in your mains. You can also use in-game currency to buy a duplicate character and wheel (weapon) of your choice (including limited) every month, which helps towards that. On balance, I would say it's effectively comparably generous to other gacha games that I've played in the broader 2d scene (more than Arknights, less than CZN and BD2), but it [i]feels[/i] better.

You can see it from the pictures, but the art is also fairly unique - I'm not aware of any other gacha games that go for the sort of soft-horror Lovecraftian aesthetic this does. Reverse 1999 is close in style minus the horror elements. Regardless, I'm a big fan.

As to the downsides - mostly pain points of a smaller project:

The translation can be rough - at some point, it was all machine translated. The main story is now human translated, but there are still a good number of minor editorial mistakes. Many of the side stories are still machine translated. New characters' cards, particularly at higher dupes, can be confusing.

The EN community is among the most unpleasant of any gacha I've played. Half of it comes down to the biggest content creator being a sort of edgy, Trumpian, discord mod type figure. If you've been on the internet long, you know the sort - they acquire a band of equally unpleasant followers and make drama. The other half comes down to the community being small enough that you do hear about them, whether you want to or not. If a Hoyo game is New York, this is more like a rural town where everyone knows each other, I suppose. 

As for the neutral: 

Each character having 5 cards + ult makes their role fairly narrow, so as with many gacha, the big decisions are in teambuilding - within combat, it doesn't feel like there's quite as much variance beyond hoping your draws are good. It's rather different than Slay the Spire in this respect, where deckbuilding itself is more varied, and since the deck can be bigger, there's more uncertainty there as well. CZN would go somewhere in the middle, and I'd say has more interesting gameplay. That's not to say the gameplay in this is bad - this is the neutral section - it's enjoyable enough, but that's all.

PvP operates under an entirely different system, and isn't p2w beyond getting a single copy of a character - both of which I appreciate since gacha PvP is so often a mess. That said, I don't like PvP, and it's unavoidable since you get a significant amount of currency from it. I will say I find it more interesting than the PvE, though I haven't played it enough to verbalize why.

Anyways, I've tried to give a balanced review, but for all my caveats, this is currently my favorite gacha off the story alone. Not that anyone actually reading this hasn't already played the game - it's too long and boring for it to appeal to anyone but the "let me see if you're wrong" demographic.

items.title: [正面] Good slay the spire gacha.
